g.text: Register Allocation for WHERE (a + b) * c > d - e
Register File State After Each Step
Key Insight: No Register Reuse = No Clobbering
Expression: WHERE (a + b) * c > d - e
R1 | Column a | Step 1: OP_COLUMN cursor,0,R1
R2 | Column b | Step 2: OP_COLUMN cursor,1,R2
R3 | a + b | Step 3: OP_ADD R1,R2,R3
R4 | Column c | Step 4: OP_COLUMN cursor,2,R4
R6 | Column d | Step 6: OP_COLUMN cursor,3,R6
R7 | Column e | Step 7: OP_COLUMN cursor,4,R7
R8 | d - e | Step 8: OP_SUBTRACT R6,R7,R8
EXPLAIN output (addr | opcode | p1 | p2 | p3 | comment)
2 | Add | R1 | R2 | R3 | a + b
7 | Subtract | R6 | R7 | R8 | d - e
8 | Le | R5 | skip | R8 | skip if (a+b)*c <= d-e
R1 (a) is never overwritten | still valid at Step 9 if needed
R3 (a+b) survives past R4 alloc | no interference between subtrees
R5 (left result) and R8 (right) | both live for final OP_LE
Tradeoff: wastes ~4 registers | eliminates entire class of bugs

text.tspan: // R1, R2 logically dead but NOT reused
// R1, R2 logically dead but NOT reused
